channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Vance oozed confidence compared to jittery Walz, body language expert says: ‘Captain of the ship’
item.description: Sen. JD Vance of Ohio and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz's body language sharply contrasted with one another at Tuesday's debate, a body language expert says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden, Harris head to hurricane-ravaged Southeast in wake of Trump visit
item.description: President Biden and Vice President Harris each travel to the storm-ravaged Southeast on Wednesday, two days after Donald Trump visited – as the death toll and devastation from Hurricane Helene soars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pennsylvania Republican in key swing-state Senate race backs using military to fight fentanyl
item.description: The Republican challenger trying to flip the U.S. Senate seat in swing-state Pennsylvania says he'll press for U.S. military action in Mexico to target fentanyl trafficking networks.
item.dc:creator: Marc Levy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Voters react to JD Vance claiming he and Donald Trump support 'clean air, clean water'
item.description: JD Vance said at the CBS Vice Presidential Debate that he and Donald Trump support "clean air, clean water," prompting mixed reactions from voters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Jersey Democrat proposes bill to create travel advisories to inform pregnant women of state abortion laws
item.description: A New Jersey Democrat proposed a bill to create travel advisories to notify women of restrictive abortion laws in other states they may be visiting.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance and Walz keep it civil in a policy-heavy discussion: VP debate takeaways
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice presidential hopefuls Tim Walz and JD Vance focused their criticism on the top of the ticket on Tuesday as they engaged in a policy-heavy discussion that may be the last debate of the 2024 presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, ZEKE MILLER and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: After pressing an Israel-Hezbollah cease-fire, the Biden administration shifts its message
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration says there is a significant difference between Israeli actions that have expanded its war against the Iranian-backed militant groups Hamas and Hezbollah and Iran’s retaliatory missile attack against Israel, which it condemned as escalatory.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some of the most notable quotes from the JD Vance-Tim Walz vice presidential debate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The first and only vice presidential debate between Ohio Senator JD Vance and Minnesota Governor Tim Walz featured an often orderly, policy-focused and even civil dialogue between the two men seeking to serve as the next president's second in command.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walz repeats Georgia abortion death falsehood decried by doctors as 'fearmongering'
item.description: Tim Walz claimed during the vice presidential debate that a Georgia woman likely died due to the state's abortion laws despite doctors slamming the narrative as "fearmongering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CBS cuts mics of Vance, Walz during immigration argument
item.description: The sparring started when Brennan clarified for debate viewers that Springfield “does have a large number of Haitian migrants who have legal status, temporary protected status.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Walz on Tiananmen Square screw-up: 'I'm a knucklehead at times'
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), pressed at the vice presidential debate over false statements he made in which he said he was present during the Tiananmen Square protests in 1989, said: “I’m a knucklehead at times.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance, Walz spar on immigration during VP debate: Been to the border 'more than our border czar'
item.description: Vice presidential candidates JD Vance and Tim Walz mixed it up on the issue of immigration during their first and only debate Tuesday night in New York City.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he would veto national abortion ban
item.description: Former President Trump on Tuesday said he would veto a federal abortion ban if it made it to his desk in a potential second term after previously refusing to say whether he would do so.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: What was the QR code on screen during the vice presidential debate?
item.description: CBS News is getting creative as it attempts to conduct Tuesday's Vice Presidential debate fairly and productively.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know on claims made about Minnesota, Walz in VP debate against Vance
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz’s home state of Minnesota came up early in his debate against fellow vice presidential candidate Ohio Sen. JD Vance on Tuesday evening. Here are the facts.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: LIVE UPDATES: Vance, Walz vice presidential debate
item.description: Vice presidential nominees J.D. Vance and Tim Walz are facing off for the first -- and likely only -- time before the 2024 election, which is just over a month away.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Are convicted felons allowed to vote in Illinois?
item.description: With just five weeks until the 2024 presidential election, most U.S. citizens over the age of 18 are eligible to vote as long as they are registered. However, for some individuals, the right to vote may be more complicated due to a criminal conviction.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, WGN 9

item.title: GOP leaders press Biden administration to get tougher on Iran after missile attack
item.description: Republican leaders on Capitol Hill are calling on President Biden to get tougher on Iran in the wake of Tuesday’s missile attack on Israel, urging the administration to adopt a series of specific measures designed to cripple the military powers of Tehran and its proxies.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats sue over controversial Georgia voting rule
item.description: Democrats filed a lawsuit Monday looking to block a controversial rule requiring ballots to be hand-counted by local precincts in Georgia, arguing it violated Georgia law and called it “improper.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Vance and Walz debate in New York City on Tuesday. Here’s what you need to know
item.description: The CBS News vice presidential debate will be the only time Ohio Sen. JD Vance and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz get to face off ahead of the 2024 election. Here's what to expect.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: 5 people killed in a 4-vehicle chain reaction crash on central Utah highway
item.description: Four people from Minnesota and a Utah man were killed in a four-vehicle chain reaction crash in central Utah. The Utah Highway Patrol says an SUV pulling onto U.S. Highway 6 near Price overshot the acceleration lane and pulled into the path of a semitractor pulling double trailers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Harris trolls Trump at Vegas rally and LA fundraiser, says her crowds are 'pretty big'
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris poked at Donald Trump over crowd sizes, his refusal to debate again and his privileged background on Sunday as she hauled in campaign cash in California and held a raucous rally at the same Nevada venue where the Republican nominee had appeared just two
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The Pentagon says it wants to prevent Iran from spreading the conflict in the Middle East
item.description: After Israel killed Hezbollah’s leader, the U.S. says it wants to stop Iran from “exploiting the situation or expanding the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster and Tom Bowman, MPR News

item.title: California Gov. Newsom vetoes AI safety bill that divided Silicon Valley
item.description: The measure, known as SB 1047, was one of the nation’s most far-reaching regulations on the booming AI industry. It would have held AI companies legally liable for harms caused by AI and enabled a "kill switch" if systems went rogue.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: What Tim Walz's past debate performances reveal about how he may take on JD Vance
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz’s track record suggests the Democratic governor will focus on his experiences pre-politics. Those who’ve debated him before say Walz has a down-to-earth style that is relatable — but that style can also cause problems for the governor.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Tim Walz’s experience in China could help him as veep, but he barely mentions it as a candidate
item.description: When Gov. Tim Walz first ran for Congress in 2006, his campaign proudly touted his extensive experience in China. In fact, earlier in his political career, he occasionally exaggerated it. But now that he’s running for vice president, Walz barely mentions China.
item.dc:creator: Forest Hunt, Caspar von Au, and Curtis Gilbert, MPR News

item.title: Kamala Harris presents case for rebuilding the middle class in Las Vegas campaign stop
item.description: Kamala Harris wrapped up her second visit to Las Vegas since she became the Democratic presidential candidate, focusing on multiple policy issues.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Peguero, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pennsylvania's Latinos could be key to deciding who wins the presidency
item.description: The Latino vote could be decisive in this year’s election. The largely Puerto Rican population in Pennsylvania has deep ties to Democrats, making efforts to engage those communities key for the party.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Lopez, MPR News

item.title: Exploring the Eric Adams indictment for bribery, wire fraud, and solicitating contributions from Turkish nationals
item.description: New York City Mayor Eric Adams pleaded not guilty in a federal bribery case after being indicted on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Johan Sheridan, WFLA, NBC

item.title: VIDEO: Donald Trump tosses chicken tenders into crowd at college football game
item.description: Former President Donald Trump surprised college students on Saturday in Alabama with an unusual gift for football fans.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Here are the rules for the Vance-Walz CBS debate
item.description: The debate, which will kick off at 9 p.m. EDT on Tuesday from the network’s New York office, will be moderated by CBS anchor Norah O’Donnell and "Face the Nation" host Margaret Brennan.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: PIX Panel discusses historic indictment against Mayor Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – History was made in New York City this week when Eric Adams became the city’s first sitting mayor to be indicted on federal charges. A federal indictment charged Adams with bribery, campaign finance and conspiracy offenses.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Senate candidates Gillibrand, Sapraicone to face off in PIX11 forum
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Election Day is just a few weeks away. Do you know who you’re voting for in the New York race for U.S. Senate? Republican candidate Mike Sapraicone is battling to unseat Democratic Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Cook, PIX 11

item.title: Nevada's Horsford calls to 'end hate' following Republican congressman's tweet calling Haitians 'thugs'
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- Democratic Nevada Rep. Steven Horsford called for the censure of a Republican U.S. House member after that congressman posted and then deleted a racist tweet.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News
